issue_title: RejectImplicitGrantExecutor does not return an error when a PAR request includes Implicit or Hybrid response type
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

oidc

### Prioritization

If this issue is not closed in time for KC 26.4, the list of supported standards in needs to revert the changes in #41348 from "FAPI 2.0 Security Profile" to "FAPI 2.0 Security Profile (Draft)".
To make this dependency visible, this is now marked as a blocker. 
It can be downgraded to "important" if the PR above is reverted.

### Describe the bug

After configureing to apply RejectImplicitGrantExecutor to a client's request, when the client sent a PAR request that includes  Implicit or Hybrid response type (other than "respose_type=code"), Keycloak returns a successful response in 201 status code.

After that, when the client send an authorization request consecutively, Keycloak returns an error response.

Indeed, Keycloak returns an error response from authorization endpoint finally, but Keycloak should return an error response from PAR endpoint in 400 status code firstly, which can omit sending an unnecessary authorization request.

### Version

26.3.2

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

1. After configureing to apply RejectImplicitGrantExecutor to a client's request, the client sent a PAR request that includes Implicit or Hybrid response type (other than "respose_type=code". e.g., "response_type=token").
2. keycloak returns an error response in 400 status code with "invalid_request" error.

### Actual behavior

1. After configureing to apply RejectImplicitGrantExecutor to a client's request, the client sent a PAR request that includes Implicit or Hybrid response type (other than "respose_type=code". e.g., "response_type=token").
2. keycloak returns an error response in 201status code with valid "request_uri" parameter.

### How to Reproduce?

1. After configureing to apply RejectImplicitGrantExecutor to a client's request, the client sent a PAR request that includes Implicit or Hybrid response type (other than "respose_type=code". e.g., "response_type=token").

### Anything else?

The OpenID Foundation's conformance suite of FAPI 2.0 Security Profile Final specification expects the expected behavior.
commit_message:
RejectImplicitGrantExecutor does not return an error when a PAR request includes Implicit or Hybrid response type

closes #41609

issue_title: Test SMTP connection fails when no port is specified
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/api

### Describe the bug

This is a regression created by #39486. When no port is specified the condition added for that issue incorrectly compares the empty string `""` with the port `25`. Check this [line](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/26.3.2/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/services/resources/admin/RealmAdminResource.java). Therefore the test thinks that the server is different (different port) and the password is not recovered from the configuration. This finishes with a login error instead of a successful test.

The root cause seems to be that the `port` is sent by the console as `null` (in json `"port": null`) and that makes `settings.getOrDefault("port", "25")` return `null`. So I will use Optional or similar.

The easy workaround is just specify the default port to `25` in the admin console.

### Version

26.3.2

### Regression

- [x] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The test should work OK.

### Actual behavior

Test fails with incorrect password as the password is not recovered from the configuration.

### How to Reproduce?

Just configure a SMTP server in default port 25 without specifying it in teh configuration. After saving , reload the smtp config and test again. It will fail with invalid credentials.

### Anything else?

_No response_

Closes #41643

issue_title: TiDB Many WAITING threads during high load scenario
issue_body: ### Before reporting an issue - [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them. ### Area core ### Describe the bug I am currently testing our KC to see how much load it can handle in the current configuration. Running on EKS using 3 dedicated c7i.xlarge machines Because some DaemonSets are running also there, this is the KC resource config: ``` resources: limits: cpu: "3" memory: 6G requests: cpu: "3" memory: 6G ``` Its a KC 26.2.5 instance database connections ``` - name: KC_DB_POOL_INITIAL_SIZE value: "25" - name: KC_DB_POOL_MAX_SIZE value: "25" - name: KC_DB_POOL_MIN_SIZE value: "25" ``` ``` KC_HTTP_MAX_QUEUED_REQUESTS=1000 KC_HTTP_POOL_MAX_THREADS=50 ``` 230 http req/sec are generated (~ 30 Logins/sec, ~30 code2token/sec,~150 refreshtoken/sec) via the Keycloak benchmark cli. It is strange that with this number of requests, the response times increase, even though the CPU of the pod is only at 80%. The database connection pool is only utilized at 15%. It seems that the requests are spending a long time in the WorkerPool Queue (since the usage duration values are very low, and queue duration values are high): That's why I increased the KC_HTTP_POOL_MAX_THREADS, unfortunately without success. Then I looked into Threads with JavaMissionControl. Seems to be that all WAITING threads are blocked by this line of code: [https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/73e5f4bead7840f19210aaabcbd1a00895d46929[…]oak/models/sessions/infinispan/changes/JpaChangesPerformer.java](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/73e5f4bead7840f19210aaabcbd1a00895d46929/model/infinispan/src/main/java/org/keycloak/models/sessions/infinispan/changes/JpaChangesPerformer.java#L109C23-L109C59) Furthermore I saw that the vert.x threads are under heavy load. Is that the newly introduced virtual threads feature for infinispan/jgroups? Any Ideas? ### Version KC 26.2.5 ### Regression - [ ] The issue is a regression ### Expected behavior CPU can be fully utilized ### Actual behavior CPU is not fully utilized, but threads are waiting on each other ### How to Reproduce? Run Keycloak Benchmark up to 250req/sec with scenario `keycloak.scenario.authentication.AuthorizationCode` on 3x 3CPU/6GB RAM KC Cluster ### Anything else? _No response_

issue_title: Keycloak SAML Adapter subsystem does not work in WildFly 37
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

adapter/jee-saml

### Describe the bug

Attempting to use the latest Keycloak SAML adapter with the [newly released WildFly 37](https://www.wildfly.org/news/2025/08/04/WildFly-37-is-released/) results in a server start failure due to a missing constructor exception.

`Caused by: java.lang.NoSuchMethodError: 'void org.jboss.as.server.deployment.module.ModuleDependency.<init>(org.jboss.modules.ModuleLoader, org.jboss.modules.ModuleIdentifier, boolean, boolean, boolean, boolean)'
  at org.keycloak.keycloak-saml-wildfly-subsystem@26.3.2//org.keycloak.subsystem.adapter.saml.extension.KeycloakDependencyProcessorWildFly.addCoreModules(KeycloakDependencyProcessorWildFly.java:41)
  at org.keycloak.keycloak-saml-wildfly-subsystem@26.3.2//org.keycloak.subsystem.adapter.saml.extension.KeycloakDependencyProcessor.deploy(KeycloakDependencyProcessor.java:71)
  at org.jboss.as.server@29.0.0.Final//org.jboss.as.server.deployment.DeploymentUnitPhaseService.start(DeploymentUnitPhaseService.java:165)`

Upon investigation, the Keycloak adapter currently relies on deprecated & marked for removal features from WildFly core, some of which were removed in the latest WildFly core release, 29.0.0.Final. Namely, `org.jboss.modules.ModuleIdentifier` and the `org.jboss.as.server.deployment.module.ModuleDependency` constructor in use in `KeycloakDependencyProcessor `& `KeycloakDependencyProcessorWildFly` with in the `keycloak-saml-wildfly-subsystem` jar.

### Version

SAML Adapter 26.3.2

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

A SAML adapter configured WildFly instance to start successfully and allow for successful SAML authentication for the deployed & configured applications.

### Actual behavior

WildFly fails to start the SAML adapter and therefore, fails to start the whole server.

### How to Reproduce?

- Configure WildFly 37 with the latest SAML adapter, e.g. through installing the feature pack using galleon
- Configure & enable the keycloak-saml subsystem and elytron as required
- Attempt to start the server

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Updated the WildFly subsystem module to avoid using deprecated functionality from wildfly-core that's been removed in the latest version

Replaced instances of the deprecated class ModuleIdentifier with strings.
For now, maintain the use of the deprecated ModuleDependency constructor (while now using the module identifier strings from above in place of the ModuleIdentifier instances) in order to maximise compatibility. This ensures support for both WildFly, up to at least 37, and EAP 8.
Moved all the module identifier strings to KeycloakDependencyProcessor so they're all in one place. Removed duplicates and update references from other classes.

Closes #41669

Signed-off-by: Peter Griffiths <peter.griffiths@1spatial.com>

### Area

### Describe the bug

Starting in Keycloak 26.3.1, any call to `searchForUserStream()` in custom providers triggers a `NullPointerException: Null keys are not supported!` due to authorization filtering attempting to lookup users with null IDs.

issue_title: Clicking email confirmation links in Outlook results in a "stale link" error
issue_body: ### Before reporting an issue - [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them. ### Area core ### Describe the bug When using the UPDATE_EMAIL feature, updating your email address in the account console and then clicking on the verification link in some email clients (at least Outlook) results in an error: The email address is still successfully updated, but the error message might be confusing for the user. --- The issue is caused by Outlook "Safe Links" [sending a HEAD request](https://community.wappler.io/t/dealing-with-head-requests/33802), which already verifies the email address. The GET request from the browser then results in a EXECUTE_ACTIONS_ERROR with reason "staleEmailVerificationLink". The verification link upon initial registration also has this issue, but in that case doesn't result in a user-visible error, because the VERIFY_EMAIL_ERROR with error "email_already_verified" still results in an automatic redirect back to the application, just as a successful VERIFY_EMAIL would. ### Version 26.2.5 ### Regression - [ ] The issue is a regression ### Expected behavior HEAD requests to the `/realms//login-actions/action-token` endpoint should not cause any state change on the server. ### Actual behavior HEAD requests to the `/realms//login-actions/action-token` endpoint complete the action and invalidate the token. ### How to Reproduce? 1. Enable the UPDATE_EMAIL feature and the corresponding "Update Email" required action. 2. Open the account console and log in to an existing account. Click the "Update email address" button 3. Enter a valid email address and click "Submit" 4. Open the email in Microsoft Outlook and click the link. (This only works with publicly accessible Keycloak instances on the internet, as the HEAD request originates from Microsoft's servers). If you don't have Outlook or your Keycloak instance isn't public, you can instead copy the verification link and manually send a HEAD request using a tool of your choice, e.g. `curl -I `, and then open the link in your browser. 5. You will see "stale link" error message. ### Anything else? Keycloak event log: nginx log: You can see the HEAD request from a Microsoft IP arriving first and getting a 200 response, then the GET request from my browser arriving second and getting a 400 response. --- The responsible code should be this: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/ef312b570c2ed0243242b93688016d7aaf6d91fd/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/services/resources/LoginActionsService.java#L532-L549 It seems that if no dedicated `@HEAD` handler is defined, the `@GET` handler is called instead (which in this case has side effects). There is an argument to be made that neither HEAD nor GET requests should actually cause an action to complete, because that violates the assumption of them being [safe](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/Safe/HTTP) and [idempotent](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/Idempotent). But to solve that, the verification link would have to always show a form with a button that sends a POST request. To solve this issue, it would be enough for HEAD requests to not execute the action token handler.
commit_message:
Skip processing HEAD requests for action tokens

Closes #41834

### Expected behavior

We have encountered a security issue with syncing LDAP groupOfNames entities to Keycloak roles with role-ldap-mapper. We have created simplest scenario that you could use to reproduce.

Issue occurs when we try to sync users that has memberOf attribute with some value outside the role DN. Using simply cn=test would lead to assigning all roles that are synced from LDAP.

### Actual behavior

Do not fetch roles (and groups) if memberOf value is empty of values are using a baseDN other than the base DN configured in the role/group mapper.

### How to Reproduce?

Issue occurs when we try to sync users that has memberOf attribute with some value outside the role DN. Using simply cn=test would lead to assigning all roles that are synced from LDAP.

### Anything else?

_No response_

memberOf attribute empty or values with a DN that does not match the role base DN fetches all roles

Closes #41842

issue_title: Hibernate 7.1 breaks TiDB support
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

ci

### Describe the bug

The test showed to be unstable in https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/actions/runs/16966315188/job/48115619560?pr=41882

```
2025-08-14T18:21:00.1129051Z 2025-08-14 18:21:00,047 WARN  [managed.keycloak] (pool-2-thread-1) [liquibase.changelog.DatabaseChangeLog] (main) Due to mysql SQL limitations, setNullable will lose primary key/autoincrement/not null/comment settings explicitly redefined in the change. Use <sql> or <modifySql> to re-specify all configuration if this is the case
2025-08-14T18:21:37.2154982Z 2025-08-14 18:21:37,209 WARN  [managed.keycloak] (main) [org.hibernate.orm.jdbc.error] (executor-thread-1) HHH000247: ErrorCode: 1146, SQLState: 42S02
2025-08-14T18:21:37.2175552Z 2025-08-14 18:21:37,209 WARN  [managed.keycloak] (main) [org.hibernate.orm.jdbc.error] (executor-thread-1) Table 'test.ue1_0' doesn't exist
2025-08-14T18:21:37.2177621Z 2025-08-14 18:21:37,209 ERROR [managed.keycloak] (main) [org.keycloak.services.error.KeycloakErrorHandler] (executor-thread-1) Uncaught server error: org.keycloak.models.ModelException: Database operation failed
```

This is caused by the Hibernate 7.1 upgrade, which creates different SQL statements which are not understood by TiDB: 

```
-- Hibernate 7.1.0.Final - fails on TiDB, succeeds on MySQL
select ue1_0.ID from USER_ENTITY ue1_0 where ue1_0.ID=1 for update of ue1_0;

-- Hibernate 7.0.8.Final
select ue1_0.ID from USER_ENTITY ue1_0 where ue1_0.ID=1 for update of USER_ENTITY;
```


### Version

main

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

A deletion of a user should succeed

### Actual behavior

A deletion of a user doesn't work

### How to Reproduce?

Run any test locally, for example UserResourceTypePermissionTest

### Anything else?

issue_title: Backwards incompatible changes to 26.3.0 cause NullPointerException when requesting /certificates/jwt.credential/generate-and-download
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

authentication

### Describe the bug

**Overview**
After upgrade of Keycloak server from 26.1.2 to 26.3.2, our services started receiving 500 when requesting keypair and certificate generation (POST `/auth/admin/realms/{realm}/clients/{client}/certificates/jwt.credential/generate-and-download`).
Our server stack trace indicates a NullPointerException in [ClientAttributeCertificateResource](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/42520d8409847d456870df75cede4993a03a6a61/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/services/resources/admin/ClientAttributeCertificateResource.java#L346):
```
"exception": {
  "refId":1,
  "exceptionType":"java.lang.NullPointerException",
  "message":"Cannot invoke \"java.lang.Integer.intValue()\" because the return value of \"org.keycloak.representations.KeyStoreConfig.getKeySize()\" is null",
  "frames":[{"class":"org.keycloak.services.resources.admin.ClientAttributeCertificateResource","method":"generateAndGetKeystore","line":346}...
```
This issue happens because of backwards incompatible changes that were introduced in scope of [this PR](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/40428/files) to solve https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/38620. The problem is that new fields `keySize` and `validity` are not nullable, but they do not have fallback values.
This change was reflected in `keycloak-admin-client` / `keycloak-client-common-synced` since 26.0.6, but previous versions do not support these fields.
So, our services that use `keycloak-admin-client` / `keycloak-client-common-synced` of older versions (26.0.4 / 26.0.5) now receive 500s.

### Version

26.3.2

### Regression

- [x] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Contract of `/auth/admin/realms/{realm}/clients/{client}/certificates/jwt.credential/generate-and-download` endpoint is updated in a backwards compatible way to allow users to update server first and then client libraries with no downtime.

### Actual behavior

Contract of `/auth/admin/realms/{realm}/clients/{client}/certificates/jwt.credential/generate-and-download` fail with 500 when new fields `keySize` and `validity` are not populated. This does not allow us to update server first and then client libraries with no downtime

### How to Reproduce?

Request keypair and certificate generation using `keycloak-admin-client` / `keycloak-client-common-synced` on the version prior to 26.0.6 against Keycloak 26.3.2.

```
var config = new KeyStoreConfig();
config.setFormat("PKCS12");
config.setKeyAlias(clientId);
config.setKeyPassword(keyPassword);
config.setStorePassword(keyPassword);

var keystore = clientResource.getCertficateResource("jwt.credential").generateAndGetKeystore(config);
```

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Check null for new keySize and validity parameters when generating certificates
Closes #41906

Signed-off-by: rmartinc <rmartinc@redhat.com>

issue_title: Role mapping `account.manage-account-links` not sufficient for Client initiated account linking
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

authentication

### Describe the bug

Role mapping `account.manage-account-links` is not sufficient to perform Client initiated account linking. Trying to link accounts will only result in an `Insufficient permissions to link identities.` error. 

The [dokumentation](https://www.keycloak.org/docs/latest/server_development/index.html#client-initiated-account-linking) states:

> The user must have an account.manage-account **or** account.manage-account-links role mapping.



### Version

26.3.2

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Client initiated account linking should be possible with just the `account.manage-account-links` role mapping. 

### Actual behavior

Client initiated account linking is only possible with the `account.manage-account` role mapping.

### How to Reproduce?

Follow the procedure outlined in [Client initiated account linking](https://www.keycloak.org/docs/latest/server_development/index.html#client-initiated-account-linking`) and only assign the `account.manage-account-links` role mapping.

### Anything else?

The check in [IdentityBrokerService.java](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/services/resources/IdentityBrokerService.java#L1004) only checks for `AccountRoles.MANAGE_ACCOUNT` and will return an INSUFFICIENT_PERMISSION error if not present.

issue_title: Check for non-ascii local part on emails depending on SMTP configuration
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

This is a follow-up for #41023. With the changes done for that issue now the SMTP configuration rejects sending emails to addresses that have non-ascii in the local part if the SMTP configuration does not allow UTF-8. This is OK because we are preventing sending messages to incorrect addresses but we have decided to add this validation to the built-in UP email validator. This way the server will also reject the registration of emails that cannot be used by the SMTP server.

We have managed two ideas:

1. Just use the SMTP configuration to control the email and allow non-ascii if SMTP is not configured or allows UTF-8.
2. Add an option in the validator. This option is independent to the SMTP configuration in the realm.

For the moment I will use the option 1 for the PR but this can change after reviews.

### Version

999

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The email with non-ascii chars in the local part should be rejected if the SMTP server configuration does not allow sending them.

### Actual behavior

The email is accepted.

### How to Reproduce?

See description.

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Check for non-ascii local part on emails depending on SMTP configuration

Closes #41994

Signed-off-by: rmartinc <rmartinc@redhat.com>

issue_title: Client session timestamp not updated in the database if running multiple nodes
issue_body: ### Before reporting an issue - [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them. ### Area core ### Describe the bug Hi everyone, I'm reaching out seeking help to solve a weird behavior that we are observing with our Keycloak instances. On Keycloak 26.2.5 or 26.3.1, every time we deploy a new version of our Keycloak with custom providers, some user sessions are losing their client sessions, even if they are not expired yet. See the following screenshot before a deployment: Then, after the Keycloak pods were restarted, one session lost its client: This is in one of our lower environments, the problem is a lot worse in production, where there are many sessions active. Then, we start to see a lot of `REFRESH_TOKEN_ERROR` in our logs: ``` type="REFRESH_TOKEN_ERROR", realmId="1234", realmName="redacted", clientId="redacted", userId="null", sessionId="4321", ipAddress="redacted", error="invalid_token", reason="Session doesn't have required client", grant_type="refresh_token", refresh_token_type="Refresh", ``` This is our session lifetime configs: We currently have two Keycloak pods running on Kubernetes ### Version 26.3.1 ### Regression - [x] The issue is a regression ### Expected behavior The user sessions should not lose their clients after each Keycloak restart, or if this is expected, a clear documentation on why it's needed and how to tweak it. ### Actual behavior Refresh of access tokens fails because user sessions are losing their client sessions during Keycloak restart. This started to happen after we upgraded from Keycloak 26.1.4 to 26.2.5. ### How to Reproduce? Unfortunately, I couldn't find a reliable way to reproduce this other than creating the OIDC sessions and restarting the application manually. ### Anything else? We also observe some logs from `ClientSessionPersistentChangelogBasedTransaction`, like: ``` client-session not imported from persister for sessionId=null, offline=false, removing from persister. ``` **Enabled features:**
